TextView.android:text: Master your money with expert advice
Start with ₹1,000, then aim for one month of expenses. Gradually build up to 3-6 months. This protects you from job loss, medical emergencies, or unexpected repairs.
Save at least 20% of your income before spending on anything else. Automate transfers to savings on payday to make it effortless.
The power of compound interest means money invested today grows exponentially over time. Even ₹500/month starting at age 25 can build significant wealth by retirement.
Focus on clearing credit card debt and personal loans with high interest rates. These grow quickly and cost you the most money over time.
Allocate 50% for Needs, 30% for Wants, and 20% for Savings/Debt. This simple ratio ensures you cover essentials while still enjoying life and building wealth.
Give every single dollar a job before the month begins. Your income minus expenses should equal zero. This eliminates mindless spending and ensures every cent works for you.
This week, focus on building or reviewing your emergency fund. Aim for ₹1,000 first, then work toward 1 month of expenses. Your future self will thank you!
Money is a Tool, Not the Goal
Shift your perspective: money is meant to serve your life goals, not just to be accumulated. Focus on what you want to achieve with it.
Curb impulse buying by waiting one full day. This gives your brain time to switch from emotional 'want' to logical 'need'.
Create a clear vision for your financial future. Whether it's a home, travel, or retirement, visualization keeps you motivated during tough saving periods.
As your income grows, maintain your current lifestyle and invest the extra. Avoiding the trap of increasing spending with every raise is the fastest way to wealth.
Use physical or digital envelopes to set strict limits for spending categories like groceries, dining, and fun. Once the money's gone, no more spending until next month!
